title: Archived Consultation on potential measures to regulate tobacco packages and products - Canada.ca
content:
## Archived Consultation on potential measures to regulate tobacco packages and products
The public consultation is now closed. Thank you for your interest.
This consultation is seeking comments on potential measures for standardizing tobacco packages and products under the Tobacco Act.
- Video - Tobacco Plain Packaging
One of the most effective ways of reducing lifetime tobacco use is to prevent its start at an early age. An important part of reducing the appeal of tobacco packages and products, particularly among young people, is to standardize their appearance.
Elements being considered include:
- use of a common: colour text size font style
- restrictions on the use of: logos trademarks
The consultation is looking for comments from:
- other interested parties, such as: partners stakeholders health professionals and associations
- health professionals and associations
The purpose of this consultation is to gather comments on the measures being considered to standardize tobacco packages and products. This includes their:
The consultation will be available at canada.ca/Health between May 31 and August 31, 2016.
The consultation document gives you information on the background and measures being considered.
You may submit your comments by:
- email , in electronic files such as: Microsoft Word Adobe Acrobat
All comments received will be reviewed and a summary of the results will be posted online.
Tobacco Products Regulatory Office Tobacco Control Directorate Address Locator 0301A, 150 Tunney's Pasture Driveway Ottawa, ON K1A 0K9
Email: hc.pregs.sc@canada.ca
